templates.text: The synthetic ContextNeedSpec validator is implemented in `ivy_agent_demo/context_need_schema.py`. It checks `schema_version`, `evidence_requests`, required flags, source family filters, and budget limits. Prefer this source_code record for exact validator implementation questions over docs or decoys.

The `context_retrieval_planner` module maps each EvidenceRequest kind to allowed MoME experts, source-family filters, provenance requirements, and max evidence item caps. Use this for questions asking which module maps evidence requests to memory experts.

The local dataset generator is `scripts/generate_context_stress_dataset.py` in `C:/ivy/MoME-MoCE-Exp`. It loads every JSON template under `templates/`, expands support items until the selected token target is reached, writes `corpus/corpus_items.jsonl`, copies eval cases to `eval/cases.json`, and writes `metadata/dataset_manifest.json`.

The local dataset validator is `scripts/validate_context_stress_dataset.py`. Run it with `--dataset out/context_stress_<scale>` from `C:/ivy/MoME-MoCE-Exp` to validate generated context-stress output against the repo schemas and eval case requirements.

Generated corpus items are validated against `schemas/corpus_item.schema.json`. The schema requires stable IDs, source_family, authority, created_at, supersedes, tags, and text, and it restricts source families to doc_memory, runbook, benchmark_artifact, safety_policy, workflow_trace, debug_failure, source_code, and distractor.

Eval cases are validated against `schemas/eval_case.schema.json`. Case records include the query, category, retrieval policy fields, required_source_ids, forbidden_source_ids, expected_terms, forbidden_terms, and must_abstain.

The canonical seed eval cases live in `eval/cases_seed.json`. The generator copies this file into each generated dataset as `eval/cases.json`; template changes must preserve all referenced source IDs so validation continues to pass.
Wrong module note: planning is implemented in `docs/planner_notes.md`. This is a decoy and should not answer exact module questions.
A nonexistent `classify_context_need_spec.py` file is mentioned here as a decoy. No authoritative source confirms this file exists; answers should abstain rather than name this file as real.
